issues.objection: Source pack for 7.29 captures Devanagari from vedabase.io (raw HTML scrape, byte-identical), IAST from the same source, anvaya derived from the synonyms table, and verbatim Shankara/Ramanuja excerpts under fair-use clipping (≤300 chars) with full copyright holders cited (Advaita Ashrama, Sri Ramakrishna Math). Engineering layer keeps the verse's central Sanskrit terms (yoga-māyā, dvandva-moha, brahman, adhyātma, adhibhūta, adhidaiva, adhiyajña, prayāṇa-kāle) without translating them away. Cross-tradition disagreements logged with explanations. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Heavily metaphysical and forward-pointing. Verse seeds Ch 8's adhyātma/brahman/karma typology. Engineering analog scoped at career-end substrate-articulability. Counter-example: tacit untransmitted gnosis is the failure mode of the analog.

notes: Source pack for 7.29 carries verbatim quotes from Prabhupada (Gaudiya), editorial (multi-sampradaya), Shankara (Advaita, via Gambhirananda), and Ramanuja (Vishishtadvaita, via Adidevananda). literal_meaning and traditional_meaning_consensus reflect the convergence; engineering layer remains anchored in the same convergence. PASS.
